title: cassia tora var. obtusifolia
intro: Cassia tora var. obtusifolia, also known as the Indian laurel or small-leaved chakramardana, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cassia tora var. obtusifolia include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to help manage blood sugar levels and reduce oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and powder, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or topical creams for medicinal purposes.
